title: logs --lines is interpolated into a shell command
reasoning: The non-follow logs path passes the user-controlled --lines option through a template string to execSync, which invokes a shell. A value such as `--lines '10; touch /tmp/pwned'` would be parsed by the shell as an additional command. The follow path already uses spawn with an argv array, so this is an avoidable command-injection bug at the user-input/process-exec boundary.
reproduction: Run `chris logs --lines '1; touch /tmp/chris-assistant-injected'` and observe that the shell command after the semicolon can execute.
recommendation: Replace execSync with execFileSync or spawn using an argument array, e.g. `execFileSync("npx", ["pm2", "logs", PM2_NAME, "--nostream", "--lines", String(opts.lines)], { stdio: "inherit" })`. Also validate that --lines is a positive integer before passing it to pm2.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for the CLI logs command or for shell metacharacter handling in command options.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CLI unit test or child-process integration test that invokes the logs command with a malicious --lines value and asserts the pm2 invocation receives it as a single argv value or rejects it as invalid, without executing shell metacharacters.
minimumFixScope: Update `src/cli/commands/logs.ts` non-follow branch to avoid shell execution and validate `opts.lines`.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists, but the vulnerable lines no longer do. Current src/cli/commands/logs.ts validates --lines with parseLogLineCount at lines 5-16, rejects non-digits and values below 1, and the non-follow branch now calls execFileSync("npx", ["pm2", "logs", PM2_NAME, "--nostream", "--lines", lines], ...) at lines 43-46 instead of interpolating opts.lines into a shell string. The follow branch also uses spawn with an argv array. A regression test exists in tests/cli-logs-command.test.ts that rejects "1; touch /tmp/injected" and asserts execFileSync receives argv. I also checked a nearby Symphony logs command; it reuses parseLogLineCount for PM2 logs and uses argv-based spawn/execFileSync. I could not execute the test in this read-only sandbox because Vitest tried to write node_modules/.vite-temp and failed with EPERM, but the current code and test evidence show the command-injection issue is fixed.
commands: mgrep "logs command implementation where --lines is passed to pm2 or child process" src/cli
mgrep "tests for logs command lines option shell metacharacters validation pm2 logs" .
